trade.summary: The CEPA (Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement) governs bilateral trade. India exports pharmaceuticals, engineering goods, chemicals, and textiles. Giappone exports automobiles, electronics, machinery, and steel. Giappone is a key source of high-value capital goods for India's manufacturing sector.

diplomatic.summary: India and Giappone have a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership', one of India's highest bilateral designations. Both are Quad members (with Stati Uniti and Australia) aligned on Indo-Pacific stability and Cina balancing. Annual summit-level meetings are held. Giappone under Abe/Kishida prioritized India as a key partner. PM Modi and successive Giapponeese PMs have maintained very close personal diplomacy.

military.summary: As Quad partners, India and Giappone conduct regular bilateral military exercises (Malabar trilateral includes both, Dharma Guardian army exercises). Giappone has provided defence equipment through ODA-related channels. Giappone-India 2+2 foreign and defence ministerial dialogue institutionalizes the security relationship. Giappone has signed logistics support agreements with India.

financial.summary: Giappone is one of India's largest ODA providers. Giappone is building the Mumbai-Ahmedabad high-speed rail corridor (Bullet Train project). Major Giapponeese companies (Toyota, Suzuki/Maruti, Sony, Hitachi) are deeply embedded in the Indian economy. JBIC and JICA fund major Indian infrastructure. Giappone-India Investment Promotion Partnership facilitates FDI.

energy.summary: Cooperation on nuclear energy (though civil nuclear deal negotiations have been slow). Giappone is funding India's renewable energy transition. Giappone has expressed interest in LNG supply from India's planned export terminals. Cooperation on green hydrogen is growing.

scientific.summary: Deep scientific and technological cooperation. ISRO-JAXA collaboration on space missions. Joint research in robotics, AI, disaster management, and materials science. Many Indian scientists trained in Giappone. The Science and Technology Joint Committee meets regularly.
